questions.clue: This punctuation mark is used at the end of a question.
The main character of a story is known by this term.
This literary device compares two things using "like" or "as."
A word that means the opposite of another word is called this.
A word that sounds the same as another word but has a different meaning and spelling is called this.
A number divisible only by 1 and itself is called this.
This shape has eight sides.
This number sequence begins 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8...
The average of a set of numbers is also known as this.
A number that can be written as a fraction of two integers is called this.
This organelle is often called "the powerhouse of the cell."
This law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
This region beyond Neptune contains dwarf planets such as Pluto.
This type of plate boundary is responsible for the Southern Alps and many of New Zealand's earthquakes.
This Russian scientist is credited with creating the modern periodic table.
This document, signed in 1840, is considered New Zealand's founding agreement.
This term describes a person who moves permanently to a new country.
This term describes the beliefs, customs, and way of life shared by a group of people.
This New Zealand government branch is responsible for interpreting and applying the law.
New Zealand uses this voting system, commonly shortened to MMP.
This language is the most widely spoken first language in the world.
Another name for New Zealand is this.
This French word means "Sorry"
The only official language of Brazil.
This Spanish phrase literally translates to "good day."
This exercise involves moving your body up and down from a plank position.
This food guide was once commonly shown as a sectioned triangle.
This test requires students to run back and forth between two lines before a series of timed beeps.
This vitamin is produced when your skin is exposed to sunlight.
This term describes the body's ability to take in and use oxygen during exercise.
This colour is made by mixing red and blue paint.
This painting by Leonardo da Vinci is displayed in the Louvre and is famous for its smile.
This artist painted the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel.
This Dutch artist famously cut off part of his ear.
This art movement, associated with artists such as Salvador Dalí, featured dreamlike and often bizarre imagery.
This tool is used to drive screws into wood.
This manufacturing process involves joining materials together by melting a filler metal.
This type of drawing shows an object as a three-dimensional sketch while keeping parallel lines parallel.
This machine-controlled manufacturing process uses computer instructions to cut or shape materials.
This programming concept allows a section of code to call itself.
This process produces four genetically unique sex cells from one parent cell.
This number identifies how many protons are found in the nucleus of an atom.
This phenomenon causes light from distant galaxies to shift toward longer wavelengths, providing evidence that the universe is expanding.
This enzyme is responsible for unwinding DNA during replication.
This quantum principle states that it is impossible to simultaneously know both the exact position and momentum of a particle.
This Roman city was destroyed by the eruption of Mount Vesuvius in 79 AD.
This agreement signed in 1919 officially ended World War I.
This king of Macedon created one of the largest empires of the ancient world before the age of 33.
This Byzantine capital fell to the Ottoman Empire in 1453.
This Carthaginian general famously crossed the Alps with elephants.
This term describes the amount of money earned from a job before tax is deducted.
This term describes owning a small part of a company.
This New Zealand government department is responsible for collecting tax. (No acronyms)
This document shows a business's income, expenses, and profit over a period of time.
This economic term describes the total value of goods and services produced within a country in a year. (The full expansion, not the acronym)
This decimal classification system is used by many libraries to organize nonfiction books.
This ancient library in Egypt was one of the largest and most famous of the ancient world.
In this Louis Sachar novel, Stanley Yelnats is sent to Camp Green Lake.
This William Golding novel follows a group of stranded schoolboys on an island.
This New Zealand-born author wrote The Whale Rider.

finalJeopardy.clue: This student of Plato taught Alexander the Great and later founded the Lyceum in Athens.
